data.title: Zoom Operating Chief to Leave for Intel Executive Role
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Zoom Communications Chief Operation Officer Aparna Bawa is departing to become chief legal and people officer at Intel.

Bawa will leave Zoom on May 8, the video-conferencing company said in a Thursday filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Intel said Bawa will lead its legal, ethics, compliance, people and culture organizations, reporting directly to CEO Lip-Bu Tan.

Bawa has served as Zoom's operating chief since May 2020, when traffic to the company surged in the wake of social distancing policies spurred by the Covid-19 pandemic.

At Zoom, Bawa's total compensation was $1.2 million in 2025.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Public Storage Launches $500 Million 5.000% Senior Notes Due 2035; Proceeds to Refinance Debt
data.description:
Public Storage entered into an underwriting agreement for $500 million of senior notes due 2035, to be issued by Public Storage Operating Company and guaranteed by the company. The notes will carry a 5.000% coupon, be issued at 99.182% of par, and pay interest semiannually on June 15 and December 15, starting June 15, 2026. The offering, made off an effective shelf registration, is expected to close on April 6, 2026. Net proceeds will repay borrowings under the revolving credit facility and fund general corporate and investment needs.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Underwriting agreement for $500 million senior notes due 2035
  • Counterparty: BofA Securities and J.P. Morgan Securities, as joint book-running managers
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 01 2026 / same
  • Reason: Refinance credit facility and fund general corporate and investment needs

Original SEC Filing: Public Storage [ PSA ] - 8-K - Apr. 02, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Norfolk Southern Renews $498.7 Million HQ Lease Financing; BA Leasing Becomes Sole Counterparty
data.description:
Norfolk Southern renewed and restructured the financing and lease of its Atlanta headquarters, executing amended and restated agreements totaling approximately $498.7 million. BA Leasing BSC now serves as the sole counterparty, with Bank of America as administrative agent under the participation agreement. The five-year triple-net lease bears floating interest at Term SOFR plus a margin and will be accounted for as a finance lease. Norfolk Southern also issued an unconditional guaranty of its railway subsidiary’s obligations, and must elect to extend, purchase, or arrange a sale at least 90 days before the end of the term.

Agreement 1: Norfolk Southern Renews $498.7 Million HQ Lease Financing; BA Leasing Becomes Sole Counterparty

  • Agreement type: Amended and Restated Participation Agreement for HQ lease financing
  • Counterparty: BA Leasing BSC and Bank of America (administrative agent)
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 01 2026 / Apr 01 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 5 years
  • Reason: Renew and streamline HQ lease financing with sole counterparty

Agreement 2: Norfolk Southern Signs Five-Year Triple-Net Finance Lease for Atlanta HQ With BA Leasing

  • Agreement type: Amended and Restated Lease (triple-net) with finance lease treatment
  • Counterparty: BA Leasing BSC
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 01 2026 / Apr 01 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 5 years
  • Reason: Renew Atlanta HQ occupancy under updated financing terms

Agreement 3: Norfolk Southern Issues Unconditional Guaranty Supporting HQ Lease Obligations

  • Agreement type: Amended and Restated Guaranty of lease obligations
  • Counterparty: BA Leasing BSC
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 01 2026 / Apr 01 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 5 years
  • Reason: Backstop subsidiary obligations under renewed HQ lease

Original SEC Filing: NORFOLK SOUTHERN CORP [ NSC ] - 8-K - Apr. 02, 2026

data.title: Investcorp Credit Management BDC reports Q4 2025 NAV $4.25; net assets $61.3M, NII $1.9M
data.description:
Investcorp Credit Management BDC reported results for the quarter and year ended December 31, 2025, with net assets of $61.3 million and net asset value per share of $4.25 as of December 31, 2025. For the twelve months ended December 31, 2025 the company reported net investment income after taxes of $1.905 million and net investment income before taxes of $2.353 million, and a net decrease in net assets from operations of $8.848 million for the year. The board has initiated a review of strategic alternatives led by a Special Committee of independent directors and decided not to declare a quarterly dividend for the quarter ended March 31, 2026.

Financial Highlights

  • Total assets: $188.8 million as of December 31, 2025; investment portfolio at fair value: $172.7 million.
  • Net assets: $61.3 million as of December 31, 2025; net asset value per share: $4.25 (down from $5.04 at Sept. 30, 2025).
  • Net investment income before taxes (NII): $2.352 million for the twelve months ended December 31, 2025; after-tax NII: $1.905 million.
  • Total realized loss and change in unrealized appreciation (depreciation) on investments: $(10.753) million for the twelve months ended December 31, 2025, driving a net decrease in net assets resulting from operations of $(8.848) million for the year.
  • Debt, net: $123.13 million as of December 31, 2025; subsequent refinancing arranged with an affiliate providing a $65.0 million unsecured note at SOFR + 5.50% maturing July 1, 2029 to repay 2026 notes.

Business Highlights

  • Portfolio composition as of Dec. 31, 2025: investments in 37 portfolio companies; 80.76% first lien debt and 19.24% equity/warrants/other; debt portfolio ~98% floating rate.
  • Quarter activity: $1.5 million invested in one existing portfolio company; no new portfolio company investments during the quarter; total capital invested in existing portfolio companies during the quarter: $3.9 million (includes delayed draw/revolving credit advances and PIK).
  • Realizations and liquidity: proceeds from repayments, sales and amortization totaled $10.3 million in the quarter (including full realizations of three investments totaling $8.2 million); subsequent period through March 30, 2026 realized approximately $13.3 million from sale and repayment of four positions.
  • Credit activity: net draws of $1.8 million on delayed draw and revolving credit commitments to portfolio companies during the quarter; as of Dec. 31, 2025 the company had $41.1 million of unused capacity under its revolving credit facility with Capital One and $15.0 million in cash (including $10.4 million restricted).
  • Corporate actions: Board has formed a Special Committee of independent directors to evaluate strategic alternatives to maximize shareholder value; decision made not to declare a quarterly dividend for the March 31, 2026 quarter while the review is ongoing.

Original SEC Filing: Investcorp Credit Management BDC, Inc. [ ICMB ] - 8-K - Apr. 02, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Brookfield Asset Management to hold May 7, 2026 annual meeting; board slate, auditor, pay and equity plans on ballot
data.description:
Brookfield Asset Management announced its Notice of Annual Meeting for May 7, 2026 and highlighted record 2025 results, strategic deals and a 15% dividend increase.

Key Highlights:

  • Annual and special meeting set for May 7, 2026 at 2:30 p.m. (New York time) in New York and via webcast.
  • Shareholders will vote to elect 12 directors, reappoint Deloitte as auditor and approve executive compensation advisory vote.
  • Proposals include approval of a 2026 management share option plan (20M shares) and amendments to increase escrowed stock plan to 15M shares.
  • 2025: firmwide records — $112B raised, $66B deployed, fee-bearing capital $603B; fee-related earnings $3.0B; distributable earnings $2.7B.
  • Board approved a 15% dividend increase to $2.01 per share annualized and disclosed strategic initiatives including Oaktree stake acquisition and AI infrastructure funding.

data.title: Azenta Weighs Options After Thelema Fails to Secure Acquisition Financing in Time
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Azenta is evaluating paths forward for its medical refrigeration business after Thelema said it had not yet secured the financing needed to complete its proposed $63 million acquisition.

Thelema on March 27 informed Azenta that the financing was not in place, according to a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission. The transaction for B Medical Systems did not close as expected on Tuesday, the company said, with Thelema saying it needed additional time to line up its financing.

"The parties have not amended or terminated the agreement; however, the company is evaluating potential paths forward with respect to the transaction while Thelema continues to complete its financing arrangements," Azenta said.

B Medical Systems is a Luxembourg-based business that manufactures and distributes medical refrigeration devices.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Regional Health Properties 10-K: Revenues $53.16M, EPS $1.09
data.description:
Regional Health Properties reported consolidated revenue of $53.16 million for the year, driven by expanded Healthcare Services and the addition of Pharmacy Services, and delivered net income of $3.37 million, or $1.09 per share, reflecting post‑merger gains and preferred dividend impacts. Revenue grew sharply year‑over‑year as the company shifted properties to operated models and integrated the SunLink acquisition. Management attributes improved operating income partly to a $2.706 million gain on an asset sale and a bargain purchase gain recorded post‑merger.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $53.160 million, up $34.825 million (189.9%) year‑over‑year, driven by Healthcare Services expansion and addition of Pharmacy Services.
  • Gross profit: $(1.042) million (Total revenues $53.160M less Total expenses $54.202M), reflecting higher operating costs including patient care and cost of goods sold.
  • Income from operations: $1.664 million, improving $1.503 million (933.5%) year‑over‑year; includes a $2.706 million gain on asset sale.
  • Net income: $3.370 million, improved $6.588 million year‑over‑year (from a $(3.218)M loss to $3.370M) driven by a bargain purchase gain.
  • Net income (loss) per share attributable to Regional Health Properties — Basic/Diluted: $1.09, reflecting post‑merger earnings and the impact of preferred dividends.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and channel shift: The company transitioned multiple properties from leased to operated, expanding its Healthcare Services footprint and reducing reliance on real estate rental income.
  • M&A and strategic integration: Completed the SunLink merger (August 14, 2025), adding Pharmacy Services, contributing material post‑merger cash and synergy opportunities.
  • Operational cost focus: Expanded operations increased labor and other operating expenses; management is prioritizing occupancy, script counts, collections, and labor management to drive operational recovery.
  • Asset optimization: Sold Coosa Valley for $10.6 million, restructured leases, and moved underperforming assets into the operated model to improve performance.

Original SEC Filing: REGIONAL HEALTH PROPERTIES, INC [ RHEP ] - 10-K - Apr. 02, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Tigo Energy Files 8K - Entry Into Definitive Agreement >TYGO
data.description:
Tigo Energy Inc. (TYGO) filed a Form 8K - Entry Into a Definitive Agreement - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on April 02, 2026.

On March 31, 2026, Tigo Energy, Inc. (the "Company") entered into a revolving credit facility (the "Credit Facility") among the Company, as borrower, Tigo Energy MergeCo, Inc., a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Company ("Tigo MergeCo"), as guarantor, and Wells Fargo Bank, National Association, as lender.

The obligations of the Company under the Credit Facility are guaranteed by Tigo MergeCo.

Aggregate commitments under the Credit Facility total up to $10.0 million. Borrowings under the Credit Facility may not exceed the Borrowing Base amount (as defined in, and more fully described in, the Credit Facility) which is a function of the values from time to time of the Company's accounts receivables and inventory levels (as more fully described in the Credit Facility). As of the date hereof, no loans were outstanding under the Credit Facility. The Credit Facility matures on March 31, 2029.

Loans outstanding under the Credit Facility will bear interest at a rate equal to SOFR (as defined in the Credit Facility) plus an applicable margin at the per annum rate of 1.75% to 2.00% based on the Monthly Average Excess Availability (as defined in the Credit Facility).

The Credit Facility contains certain customary affirmative and negative covenants (subject in certain cases to various exceptions and carve-outs), representations and warranties and events of default (subject in certain cases to customary grace and cure periods).

The occurrence of an event of default under the Credit Facility could result in the termination of the commitments under the Credit Facility and the acceleration of all outstanding borrowings under the Credit Facility. The terms of the Credit Facility require the Company to maintain a minimum Liquidity (as defined in the Credit Facility) at all times, which is tested on a monthly basis.

The foregoing description of the Credit Facility does not purport to be complete and is subject to, and qualified in its entirety by, the full text of the Credit Facility, which is filed as Exhibit 10.1 to this Current Report on Form 8-K (this "Report") and incorporated herein by reference.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1855447/000121390026039446/ea0284556-8k_tigo.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1855447/000121390026039446/0001213900-26-039446-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Starbucks Files 8K - Regulation FD >SBUX
data.description:
Starbucks Corp. (SBUX) filed a Form 8K - Regulation FD Disclosure - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on April 02, 2026.

As previously disclosed on November 3, 2025, Starbucks entered an agreement to form a joint venture with Boyu Capital. On April 2, 2026, Starbucks announced that, following the satisfaction of all necessary closing conditions, it completed the transaction. Pursuant to the transaction, funds managed by Boyu Capital have acquired a 60% interest in Starbucks retail operations in China. Starbucks retains a 40% interest and will serve as the owner and licensor of the Starbucks global brand.

A copy of the press release issued is furnished as Exhibit 99.1 to this Form 8-K.

The information contained in Item 7.01 of this report, including the information in Exhibit 99.1, is furnished pursuant to Item 7.01 of Form 8-K and shall not be deemed "filed" for the purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, or otherwise subject to the liabilities of that section. Furthermore, the information in Item 7.01 of this report, including the information in Exhibit 99.1 attached to this report, shall not be deemed to be incorporated by reference in the filings of Starbucks Corporation under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/829224/000082922426000064/sbux-20260402.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/829224/000082922426000064/0000829224-26-000064-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Oil prices soar, and stocks end mixed in volatile trade
data.description:
Key points:
  • Some Wall Street indexes turn higher in late trading
  • Oil surges as stagflation risks swirl, denting risk assets
  • Stocks lower as Trump speech provides little clarity

By Chris Prentice and Marc Jones

Oil prices surged on Thursday and equities markets around the world were mixed in volatile trade as traders weighed conflicting developments and remarks related to the Iran war.

European shares trimmed losses, as some major Wall Street indexes and U.S. bond prices clawed back gains on news that Iran was drafting a protocol with Oman to monitor traffic in the Strait of Hormuz.

But world oil prices surged nearly 8% and U.S. crude soared more than 11% the day after U.S. President Donald Trump said in a prime-time address that the U.S. would hit Iran "extremely hard" in the coming weeks and "bring them back to the Stone Ages where they belong."

On Wall Street, stocks ended mixed in a choppy last trading day of the week ahead of the Good Friday holiday.

Gold prices fell as the U.S. dollar gained.

Government bond yields jumped on expectations that an inflation spike would force central banks to raise interest rates, or at least keep them on hold.

The dollar index , which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen and the euro, climbed 0.44%.

"Over the past 48 hours, Tehran and Washington have exchanged a cacophony of statements, some suggesting rising odds of de-escalation," BCA Research's Felix-Antoine Vezina-Poirier said. "Our GeoMacro strategists offer simple guidance for weighing volatile headlines: Stick to the facts. First, shipping through Hormuz has picked up over the past few days. Second, Iran is deliberately shifting away from GCC (Gulf Cooperation Council) targets toward Israeli ones."

WALL STREET POINTS LOWER

MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe EURONEXT:IACWI fell 0.35% to 993.18.

On Wall Street, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.13% to 46,504.67, the S&P 500 reversed course to gain 0.11% to 6,582.69 and the Nasdaq Composite added 0.18% to 21,879.18.

In a closely watched address on Wednesday, Trump said U.S. attacks on Iran would be intensified over the next two to three weeks. That came just a day after he told Reuters the U.S. would be "out of Iran pretty quickly."

The pan-European STOXX 600 index and Europe's broad FTSEurofirst 300 index both lost 0.2%.

South Korea's Kospi index slid 4.7%.

"The only thing that really matters is whether the Strait of Hormuz will open soon," said Prashant Newnaha, senior rates strategist at TD Securities.

Trump earlier said on Wednesday the U.S. did not need the key oil gateway.

Spot gold fell 1.85% to $4,669.05 an ounce and U.S. gold futures settled down 2.8% at $4,679.70.

India's central bank moved to ban trading of so-called non-deliverable forwards in an effort to halt the rupee's run of record lows. The move sent the currency up 2% , although analysts questioned how long the rebound would last.

Brent futures rallied to end up 7.78% at $109.03 a barrel, as U.S. West Texas Intermediate settled up 11.41% to $111.54.

"The fact that we can expect 2-3 more weeks of action, boots on the ground were not ruled out (during Trump's TV address) and that threats to hit infrastructure were reiterated, will put the market back on the defensive," Pictet Asset Management's Jon Withaar said.

The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes fell 1.6 basis point to 4.305%. The two-year note (US2YT=RR) yield, which typically moves in step with interest rate expectations for the Federal Reserve, was flat at 3.803%.

Euro zone benchmark Bund yields snapped a three-day decline and traders raised bets for interest-rate hikes. The yield on the benchmark German 10-year (DE10YT=RR) rose 0.1 basis points to 2.996%.

data.title: Tesla Reports 358,023 Deliveries, Misses Expectations for Second Straight Quarter
data.description:
Tesla has reported a first-quarter delivery figure that could add pressure to its near-term outlook, as the company continues to navigate a more complex electric-vehicle landscape. The automaker delivered 358,023 vehicles globally, falling short of the 372,160 analysts had been expecting, based on estimates compiled by Bloomberg, marking a second consecutive quarter of underperformance relative to expectations. While deliveries still rose 6.3% year over year, that comparison comes against a prior period impacted by Model Y production pauses and consumer backlash tied to Chief Executive Officer Elon Musk, making the latest quarter one of the company's weakest since mid-2022 outside of that disruption.

The softer delivery print comes as Tesla's core automotive business faces multiple headwinds that could weigh on demand. The US market, Tesla's largest, is entering a new phase after the expiration of federal EV incentives at the end of September, which had previously pulled demand forward into last year's second half. At the same time, policy direction under President Donald Trump has shifted away from EV support, including efforts to eliminate tax credits and weaken emissions and fuel-efficiency requirements, potentially prompting automakers to reinvest in gas-powered vehicles. Tesla is also contending with rising competition from Chinese EV manufacturers globally and an aging vehicle lineup, with plans to discontinue the Model S and Model X.

Looking deeper into the quarter, Tesla produced 408,386 vehicles, exceeding its delivery volume and potentially signaling a near-term imbalance between supply and demand. The Model Y and Model 3 remained the backbone of the business, accounting for 341,893 deliveries, while other vehicles, including the Model S, Model X, and Cybertruck, contributed 16,130 units. Meanwhile, the company's energy storage segment, which has been a relatively steady contributor, declined year over year with deployments of 8.8 gigawatt hours compared with 10.4 gigawatt hours a year earlier. As Tesla approaches its April 22 earnings report, investors could remain focused on whether the company can stabilize its core automotive performance even as it continues to emphasize longer-term initiatives such as autonomous driving, artificial intelligence, and the planned Cybercab robotaxi platform.

data.title: Navan 10-K: Revenue $702.3M, EPS (Loss) $(4.07)
data.description:
Navan reported fiscal revenue of $702.3 million, a 31% year-over-year increase driven by growth in GBV and payment volumes, while basic and diluted net loss per share was $(4.07). Despite rising revenue and a 36% increase in gross profit, the company recorded a wider net loss of $(398.0) million reflecting large non-operational charges including loss on extinguishment of debt and fair value losses. Operating losses narrowed compared with the prior year but increased operating investments continue to weigh on profitability.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $702.3 million, up 31% year-over-year.
  • Gross Profit: $500.5 million, up 36% year-over-year; gross margin 71% (up from 68%).
  • Loss from operations: $(196.9) million, improved versus prior year loss of $(107.6) million.
  • Net loss: $(398.0) million, widened from $(181.1) million; includes $117.98 million loss on extinguishment of debt and $47.04 million fair value losses.
  • Basic and diluted net loss per share: $(4.07); weighted-average shares 97.8 million.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Growth driven by a 38% increase in gross booking value (GBV) and a 13% rise in payment volume.
  • Channel and product strategy: Expanding both sales-led and product-led channels with rising adoption of Expense Management and Corporate Payments.
  • Brand and integration: Retired the Reed & Mackay brand and began migrating R&M customers onto a unified Navan platform, prompting accelerated intangible amortization.
  • Technology and AI investment: Continued heavy investment in Navan Cloud and Navan Cognition AI to enable automated virtual agents and improve support efficiency.
  • Seasonality and customer focus: Third-quarter seasonality persists; company emphasizes customer success to deepen adoption across Travel, Payments, Meetings, and VIP offerings.

Original SEC Filing: Navan, Inc. [ NAVN ] - 10-K - Apr. 02, 2026

data.title: Verizon is coming off its best quarter in 15 years. Can it sustain that momentum?
data.description:
By Hannah Pedone

Verizon has benefited from a flight to safety and from strong initial reception to a new corporate direction. But progress 'still needs to be proven over multiple quarters.'

Verizon and other telecommunications companies are making changes to the sorts of metrics they report.

For investors seeking out safer bets this year, the defensive nature of the wireless sector has held appeal. That helped propel Verizon Communications shares to their best quarter on record - but it remains to be seen whether they can keep the gains going.

The recent telecommunications momentum shouldn't be too surprising, MoffettNathanson analyst Craig Moffett noted recently. There's been a "flight from risk" in the wake of the Iran war and jitters about artificial intelligence's impact on software-linked stocks.

There are also some more company-specific issues at play. Verizon (VZ) reported a massive subscriber beat in its last earnings report, offering hope that the company is turning things around after years of disappointing performance.

Shares of Verizon rose 23.3% in the first three months of the year, making for their largest quarterly gain since the September quarter of 2010, according to Dow Jones Market Data. The iShares U.S. Telecommunications ETF IYZ rose 16% in the latest quarter.

Under new CEO Dan Schulman, Verizon is undergoing an "operational reset," Baptista Research analyst Ishan Majumdar told MarketWatch. He said that Schulman's focus on cost-cutting and simplifying plans for customers could help inject stability into the business if Verizon executes well.

"While it's far too premature to wave the victory flag" or "get complacent on wireless-industry health," Evercore ISI analyst Kutgun Maral told MarketWatch that he sees positive early signs that the company's new strategy is bearing fruit. Verizon's recent financial outlook, he added, is "more than reassuring."

But assessing Verizon's progress will be a murkier task going forward, as telecommunications companies are moving to change the way they present metrics to investors.

Verizon recently announced that the company will no longer report wireless and wireline revenues separately, nor separate business and consumer subscriber metrics, Moffett noted. T-Mobile (TMUS) also said it will no longer report individual subscriber numbers, instead shifting its focus to account counts. And AT&T (T) said it will eventually stop disclosing earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization for its wireless segment.

Moffett believes these changes will "certainly not" result in greater transparency.

He said that it's ironic that Verizon and other industry stocks have been on a "(relative) tear" given the companies are walking back disclosures. Given rising oil prices and geopolitics, the subscriber reporting changes aren't front of mind for investors now, he believes. But it's still up for debate whether Verizon and its competitors will "pay a price" for what will be "sharply reduced disclosure going forward."

Majumdar added that the company's momentum "still needs to be proven over multiple quarters."

-Hannah Pedone

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Walgreens Targets $4 Billion EBITDA Under Sycamore's Turnaround Plan
data.description:
Sycamore Partners is moving quickly to reframe the earnings profile of Walgreens Boots Alliance (WBA) following its $10 billion take-private, with internal discussions pointing to a potential doubling of EBITDA over the next several years. People familiar with the matter indicated the firm is targeting around $4 billion in EBITDA, up from roughly $2 billion generated in 2024, a goal that was presented to shareholders and some employees late last year. The same materials outlined potential exit pathways, including a sale once the target is reached or dividend distributions if Sycamore decides to hold the business longer, though both Walgreens and Sycamore have declined to comment on the financial targets.

Execution appears to be centered on a mix of cost discipline and incremental revenue initiatives that could gradually rebuild profitability. The company has already implemented staffing reductions and removed some paid holidays, while also attempting to lift store sales through added product categories such as electronic cigarettes and a possible expansion into lottery ticket sales after securing licenses in Indiana. At the same time, management has indicated that reimbursement dynamics are improving, with GLP-1 drugs, previously loss-making, now contributing positively, according to comments shared internally in February. Walgreens is expected to generate about $2.5 billion in adjusted EBITDA this year on revenue exceeding $120 billion, suggesting the path toward Sycamore's longer-term target could depend on sustained operational follow-through.

That said, the broader industry backdrop may continue to present headwinds that complicate the turnaround trajectory. Walgreens' decline over the past decade has been tied to intensifying competition from online retailers and big-box chains, alongside tighter insurance reimbursements that have pressured margins across the pharmacy sector. Peers have faced similar strain, with widespread store closures and the bankruptcy of Rite Aid highlighting structural challenges in the US drugstore model. Within that context, Sycamore's plan could represent a high-stakes effort to stabilize earnings through cost actions and selective growth initiatives, though outcomes may hinge on whether recent improvements in reimbursement and in-store performance can be sustained.

data.title: Digital Infrastructure-Focused Fundraising Surges — Market Talk
data.description: Private-equity firms worldwide raised $106 billion last year for infrastructure-focused funds that invest in data centers and other digital assets as part of their strategies, according to industry researcher Preqin. The total is nearly double the $54 billion such funds collected in 2024 and represented about half of the capital raised for all infrastructure funds last year, the private market-data provider says. "This has translated into meaningful [asset] expansion for funds with digital-infrastructure assets," Preqin says of last year's robust fundraising. Those funds managed $612 billion as of last June, and had $181 billion of that total available to spend, or dry powder, Preqin adds. North America-focused funds alone managed $279.9 billion in assets and held $91.1 billion in dry powder. (luis.garcia@wsj.com; @lhvgarcia)

data.title: China Backs $1.24 Billion Zambia-Tanzania Rail Upgrade for Copper Exports
data.description:
China is stepping deeper into Africa's copper logistics backbone, and the structure of this deal says a lot about control. CMOC Group Ltd. (CMCLY) and Zijin Mining Group Co. (ZIJMF) are partnering with China Civil Engineering Construction Corp. to upgrade the 1,860-kilometer Tazara railway linking Zambia's copper belt to Tanzania's Dar es Salaam port, in a project valued at $1.24 billion. CCECC will hold an 80% stake in the joint venture, while smaller 5% stakes are allocated to participants including Jiayou International Logistics Co., alongside units of the miners and COSCO Shipping Holdings Co.. The structure suggests China is not only financing infrastructure but also positioning itself to operate and control a key export route for critical minerals.

The timing comes as the US is trying to reshape access to African resources. Washington recently reached a minerals partnership with the Democratic Republic of Congo that gives American companies preferential access to some reserves, while backing the Lobito Corridor rail project connecting the same region to Angola's Atlantic coast. Against that backdrop, the Tazara upgrade could become a competing channel for copper and cobalt flows, particularly as CMOC and Zijin remain among the dominant exporters in Congo. The parallel development of these corridors suggests supply chains for battery materials could increasingly reflect geopolitical alignment, rather than purely cost-driven logistics.

What stands out is how the project will be run after construction. Tanzania and Zambia have granted CCECC a 30-year concession to operate the railway, with partners investing based on their stakes, including Jiayou's $62.2 million contribution. The consortium plans to upgrade infrastructure, acquire locomotives and containers, and shift mineral transport away from long-distance trucking routes. This model, blending state-backed and commercial participation, could reflect a broader shift in China's Belt and Road approach toward more commercially structured projects that still maintain strategic control over logistics networks.

data.title: Regeneron Signs Deal Granting Access to TriNetX's Patient Database
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Regeneron Pharmaceuticals will connect its data cohorts to TriNetX's data network of anonymized patients as part of collaboration between the the two companies.

By connecting its large-scale genomic and proteomic data cohorts to TriNetX's network of 300 million patients, Regeneron will be able to expand its electronic health record-linked database, the Tarrytown, N.Y., company said Thursday.

As part of the deal Regeneron plans to invest up to $200 million in TriNetX

Regeneron's database is a key driver for its therapeutics pipeline, helping with drug discovery and development.

Regeneron will be able to match a portion of TriNetX's anonymized data to genomic and proteomic data generated by the Regeneron Genetics Center.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Everyone Is Building AI That Talks Like God. One Engineer Built Something That Listens Like His Grandmother
data.description:
While faith apps race to build chatbot versions of Jesus, a Jacksonville father's prayer app has recorded more than 2,500 spoken prayers and reached nearly 2 million people without a single celebrity endorsement

JACKSONVILLE, FL / ACCESS Newswire / April 2, 2026 / In a week when Israeli police blocked the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem from entering the Church of the Holy Sepulchre and Pope Leo XIV prepares to celebrate his first Easter as the first American pope, a small app out of Jacksonville is making a quiet argument: prayer does not need a building, a celebrity, or a chatbot. It just needs your voice.

ThyWord is a voice first prayer companion that listens when you pray out loud and matches you with a Bible passage that speaks to what you are feeling. Since launching on Ash Wednesday, February 18, 2026, the app has recorded more than 2,500 spoken prayers from more than 1,250 users, with more than 1,200 in the last seven days alone. Gratitude is the most common emotion expressed heading into Easter weekend, followed by Spiritual Growth, Healing, and Strength. On social media, ThyWord's content has reached nearly 2 million accounts in 30 days, with a single Scripture post generating over 275,000 views.

The growth comes at a moment when the Barna Group reports that nearly a third of American adults trust spiritual advice from AI as much as advice from a pastor, and four in ten practicing Christians say AI has helped them with prayer or Bible study. Yet only 12 percent of pastors feel comfortable teaching about AI in their congregations. Into that gap, a wave of apps has arrived offering chatbots that simulate conversations with biblical figures. ThyWord takes the opposite approach.

"Other apps ask you to text a chatbot or listen to a celebrity guide your meditation," said Ajith Joseph, Founder and CEO of ThyWord Inc. "We built something different. ThyWord listens when you pray out loud and surfaces a Scripture that speaks to what you're feeling. No AI pretending to be God. Just you talking to the one who's always been

listening."

A respected endocrinologist called it "a divine app" that helps her connect with God during her busiest days. A mother of a child on the autism spectrum was moved to tears during her first prayer session.

A grandmother's prayer. An engineer's answer.

In a faith app market where the largest players have raised over $100 million and run Super Bowl ads, ThyWord started with a much simpler origin. Joseph's grandmother prayed every day of her life and told the family that at least one person should spend time in God's presence daily. That conviction followed him across continents and career changes, but he could never live up to it consistently - not with four children and a demanding career. The idea arrived at 3 AM on a sleepless night, born from a simple realization: his spiritual life had been reduced to one hour on Sunday morning, then six days of silence.

"I call it the six days between Sundays problem," Joseph said. "You leave church feeling connected. By Monday afternoon the noise has pushed that feeling aside. By Friday it's gone. ThyWord exists for the Tuesday afternoon, not the Sunday morning."

About ThyWord Inc.

ThyWord Inc. is a Jacksonville based company building a voice first prayer companion app for Christians. Founded by Ajith Joseph, a seasoned technology entrepreneur and father of four who previously built and sold an enterprise software platform before turning his attention to faith. Previously featured in Crosswalk.com and syndicated across Salem Media radio stations.

Available on the App Store and Google Play.

App Store: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/thyword-prayer-scripture/id6758917669

Play Store: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.thywordinc.thyword

Free to download at https://app.thywordapp.com/download

Learn more at thywordapp.com.

Media Contact

Ajith Joseph, Founder and CEO

ThyWord Inc.

press@thywordapp.com | (904) 678-1735

thywordapp.com

SOURCE: ThyWord Inc.

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Clearwater Analytics CEO Sold Shares Worth Over $1.2M
data.description:
Sahai Sandeep, a Director and Chief Executive Officer, reported sales of Class A Common Stock on March 31, 2026 to cover tax withholding related to vesting Restricted Stock Units. The transactions were executed at $23.7995 per share. The total sale amount was $1,236,788. Following the reported transactions, the reporting person directly beneficially owns 1,415,619 Class A Common Stock.

data.title: Zhongji Innolight Files for Hong Kong IPO After Shares Surge Sixfold
data.description:
Zhongji Innolight is positioning itself for a potentially significant capital markets move as the Chinese optical transceiver supplier to Nvidia Corp. confidentially files for a Hong Kong listing, according to people familiar with the matter. The company, already listed in Shenzhen, is attempting to tap into sustained investor demand tied to artificial intelligence infrastructure, particularly across the data transmission layer that underpins large-scale AI deployment. This comes as companies tied to Nvidia's ecosystem continue to attract capital flows, reflecting broader conviction that the AI buildout cycle may still be in its earlier phases.

The company's recent performance appears to reinforce that narrative. Zhongji Innolight's shares have climbed roughly sixfold over the past year in Shenzhen trading, while the company reported that its 2025 net income doubled to 10.8 billion yuan ($1.6 billion). As a manufacturer of high-speed optical transceivers, the firm sits at a critical junction of AI-related demand, supplying components that are essential for moving vast amounts of data between servers. That positioning could continue to benefit from ongoing infrastructure investment, particularly as demand for compute and networking capacity expands alongside AI adoption.

Earlier discussions have indicated that Zhongji Innolight may seek to raise at least $3 billion from the Hong Kong offering, though the size and timing of the deal remain under deliberation and could change. The company is using a confidential filing route, which allows it to delay public disclosure of sensitive information until regulatory approvals are secured, a structure that has been increasingly adopted by issuers and is now under consultation for broader use by Hong Kong's exchange. For investors, the setup may represent a notable test of whether strong momentum in AI-linked supply chain names can translate into sustained demand in new listings.

data.title: Intrepid Potash Sells Ranch to HydroSource Logistics for $70M
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Intrepid Potash sold the majority of the assets associated with a ranch in New Mexico to HydroSource Logistics for $70 million.

Diversified-mineral company Intrepid said Thursday the ranch was a noncore asset in its fertilizer portfolio. The sale would allow the company to focus on its core assets and increasing its potash and Trio production to improve unit economics, it said.

"While the Ranch has been a stable source of revenue, trends in the Delaware Basin made it clear we were not the best, long-term owner of this asset," Intrepid Chief Executive Kevin Crutchfield said, noting such assets are being consolidated across West Texas and Eastern New Mexico.

The sale of Intrepid South Ranch includes about 21,793 acres of fee land, 27,858 acres associated with federal grazing leases and water rights located on the ranch, among other assets. The assets comprise the majority of Intrepid's operations in its oilfield-solutions segment.

Intrepid said the purchase price includes an $8 million deposit it received in December.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Broadcom Appoints Thuener CFO, Succeeding Spears
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Broadcom appointed Amie Thuener as its next finance chief, succeeding Kirsten Spears, who is retiring.

Thuener joins the semiconductor and software company from Alphabet, where she has served as corporate controller and chief accounting officer since 2018. Her appointment as Broadcom's CFO is effective June 12.

Before Alphabet, Thuener was a managing director at PricewaterhouseCoopers.

"We are pleased to welcome Amie to Broadcom," Chief Executive Hock Tan said. "She will bring deep experience in financial reporting, corporate governance, AI-related transactions and leading complex, global organizations to the company as we continue to focus on creating value for our shareholders."

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Volvo Reports 29% Americas Sales Drop as Iran War Hits Demand
data.description:
Volvo Car AB said the war in Iran is starting to filter into demand, offering one of the clearest early signals that rising fuel costs could be reshaping consumer behavior in the US. The company reported an 11% decline in global sales for the first quarter, with a sharper 29% drop in the Americas, where its portfolio leans toward larger, fuel-intensive SUVs like the XC90. Management indicated that weaker customer sentiment in the region was exacerbated by the ongoing geopolitical conflict in the Middle East, suggesting that higher pump prices may be weighing on big-ticket purchasing decisions rather than just mobility costs.

The slowdown appears to be broader across the industry. BMW AG reported a 17% decline in US passenger car sales during the same period, though it did not specify the drivers behind the drop. General Motors Co. and Honda Motor Co. also posted weaker deliveries in the US, reinforcing the view that the world's second-largest auto market may be entering a softer demand phase. Taken together, the data could point to a combination of rising fuel prices, pressured sentiment, and delayed discretionary purchases, dynamics that have historically surfaced during periods of energy volatility.

For Volvo, this demand pressure comes as the company is already working through a complex recovery path. Tariffs, heavy discounting, and a stronger Swedish krona weighed on its fourth-quarter profitability at the end of 2025, and the company remains in discussions with US authorities over data protection approvals tied to continued market access. With geopolitical tensions now feeding into both demand and cost structures, the near-term outlook for automakers could increasingly depend on macro conditions rather than purely product-driven momentum.

data.title: JPMorgan Buys Short-Term Bonds After 3.1% Global Selloff
data.description:
JPMorgan Asset Management is moving back into government bonds across the US and UK, arguing that the recent inflation-driven selloff may have created pockets of value, particularly in shorter-dated debt. Arjun Vij, a Hong Kong-based portfolio manager helping oversee the firm's global bond fund, pointed to the two-to-five-year segment as one of the hardest-hit areas following aggressive repricing tied to higher rate expectations after the Iran war. That repositioning comes after the fund delivered more than 14% over the three years to February, according to a factsheet, and reflects a view that some parts of the curve could now offer more attractive entry points despite ongoing volatility.

The shift in rate expectations has been abrupt. Yields on two-year US Treasuries climbed to their highest levels since June after oil-driven inflation concerns pushed traders to price in potential Federal Reserve hikes, marking a sharp turnaround from earlier expectations of rate cuts. Comments from Donald Trump signaling a more aggressive stance toward Iran have added another layer of uncertainty, raising concerns about further disruptions to global oil supply. The broader impact has been significant, with a Bloomberg gauge of global bond returns falling 3.1% in March, its steepest monthly drop since October 2024, while shorter-dated yields in markets such as Australia have also surged.

For Vij, the dislocation could present an opportunity, but one that still requires a measured approach. He indicated that while some value may now be emerging after the shift from expected rate cuts to scenarios including hikesparticularly in the UKthe risk of further repricing at the front end is not fully behind the market. As a result, the strategy centers on gradually adding exposure while preserving liquidity, rather than deploying capital all at once. The approach reflects a balance between recognizing improved valuations and maintaining flexibility as geopolitical risks and rate expectations continue to evolve.

EQUITIES

GLOBAL - Oil prices surged on Thursday and global equity markets retreated after remarks by U.S. President Donald Trump dashed hopes of a swift resolution to the Iran war.

MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe EURONEXT:IACWI fell 0.59% to 990.80.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

NEW YORK - U.S. stocks ended slightly mixed on Thursday after paring deeper losses, as diplomatic signals from the Middle East helped calm markets rattled earlier by U.S. President Donald Trump's threats of tougher action against Iran ahead of a long holiday weekend.

According to preliminary data, the S&P 500 gained 7.26 points, or 0.11%, to end at 6,582.58 points, while the Nasdaq Composite gained 35.92 points, or 0.18%, to 21,876.87. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 61.23 points, or 0.13%, to 46,504.51.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SHANGHAI - Hong Kong's stock and bond markets, foreign exchange and commodity futures markets will be closed from Friday, April 3, for the Easter holiday.

Markets will resume trade on Wednesday, April 8.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

AUSTRALIA - There will be no Australia and New Zealand stock market report on Friday, April 3, and Monday, April 6, as both markets will be closed for Good Friday and Easter Monday holidays, respectively.

Reuters will resume coverage of the Australia and New Zealand stock markets on Tuesday, April 7.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SEOUL - South Korean shares closed more than 4% lower on Thursday as hopes faded that the Iran war could end soon after U.S. President Donald Trump vowed to continue hitting targets.

The benchmark KOSPI closed down 244.65 points, or 4.47%, at 5,234.05, after steep losses triggered a sidecar trading curb during the session.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

FOREIGN EXCHANGE

NEW YORK - The dollar rose sharply from two straight sessions of losses on Thursday after U.S. President Donald Trump's speech on Iran undermined market expectations of a swift end to the conflict, renewing a bid for safe-haven assets.

The dollar index , which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies, climbed 0.46% to 100.02.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SHANGHAI - Hong Kong's stock and bond markets, foreign exchange and commodity futures markets will be closed from Friday, April 3, for the Easter holiday.

Markets will resume trade on Wednesday, April 8.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

AUSTRALIA - The Australian and New Zealand dollars retreated anew on Thursday as markets scaled back expectations for a quick end to the Middle East conflict, slugging equities and bonds as stagflation fears intensified.

The Aussie quickly slipped 0.6% to $0.6882 , undoing a 0.4% bounce overnight. Major support lies at the 10-week low of $0.6834, and a break of that would resume the retreat toward $0.6700.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SEOUL - South Korean won weakened against dollar on Thursday.

The won was quoted at 1,519.7 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.42% lower than its previous close at 1,513.3.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TREASURIES

NEW YORK - U.S. Treasuries recouped early losses to trade modestly higher on Thursday, as investors weighed comments by President Donald Trump that dampened hopes for a quick end to war in the Middle East against news that Iran could be moving to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

In afternoon trading, benchmark 10-year yields (US10YT=TWEB) were down 1.6 bps at 4.305%, after an earlier rise triggered by Trump's speech. For the week, 10-year yields have fallen about 13 bps, on pace for their largest weekly drop since the week of February 9.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

LONDON - Euro zone bond yields were set for a weekly fall on Thursday after moving higher early in the day on the latest headlines about the Iran conflict, with traders seeking to position ahead of a long weekend.

Germany's 10-year Bund yield, the benchmark for the euro zone, was last at 2.99% a fraction lower on the day, having earlier traded as high as 3.05%.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TOKYO - Japanese government bond yields jumped on Thursday, after U.S. President Donald Trump dashed hopes for a swift end to the Middle East war, hurting the outcome of the auction for benchmark 10-year bonds.

The 10-year JGB yield (JP10YTN=JBTC) jumped 9 basis points (bps) to 2.39%, matching its highest level since February 1999, scaled on Monday.

For a full report, click on

COMMODITIES

GOLD

There will be no precious metals report on Friday, April 3, as most markets will be closed for the Good Friday holiday.

Reuters will resume coverage of the report on Monday, April 6.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

IRON ORE

Iron ore futures prices slid to their lowest in nearly three weeks on Thursday, pressured by shrinking steel margins and faltering demand following the completion of pre-holiday restocking in top consumer China.

The most-traded iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) slipped 1.29% to 805 yuan ($116.88) a metric ton, after touching its lowest since March 12 at 793.5 yuan earlier in the session.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

BASE METALS

Copper fell for the first time in five sessions on Thursday on renewed investor concerns over economic growth after U.S. President Donald Trump vowed more strikes on Iran and gave no specific timeline to end the Middle East conflict.

Benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange was down 0.7% at $12,349.50 per metric ton as of 1600 GMT. The metal, considered a bellwether for the global economy, was set to end the Easter holiday-shortened week up 1.3% after its earlier run of four straight daily gains.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

OIL

U.S. oil prices settled more than 11% higher and Brent soared nearly 8% on Thursday in volatile trading, as traders worried about prolonged disruptions to oil supply the day after President Donald Trump said the United States would continue attacks on Iran.

Brent crude futures closed $7.87, or 7.78%, higher at $109.03 a barrel. U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures rose $11.42, or 11.41%, at $111.54 per barrel, settling at their biggest absolute price rise since 2020.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

PALM OIL

Malaysian palm oil futures rose on Thursday, after declining in the previous session, buoyed by uncertainty around the outcome of the Middle East conflict and expectations of a drop in March output.

The benchmark palm oil contract for June delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange gained 25 ringgit, or 0.52%, to 4,794 ringgit ($1,187.81) a metric ton at the close.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

RUBBER

Japanese rubber futures rose on Thursday on a weaker yen, surging oil and a volatile Nikkei, after President Donald Trump failed to outline a clear end to the Iran war.

The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for September delivery , was up 5.6 yen, or 0.21%, at 387.6 yen ($2.43) per kg.
